ksa_text: Knowledge of MLOps principles and practices, including CI/CD for ML, automated retraining pipelines, model versioning, A/B testing infrastructure, and canary deployment patterns.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: AI risks and benefits from third-party entities are managed.

top_anchors.element_label: AI risks and benefits from third-party entities are managed.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain AI/ML Engineering KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.434) to justify inclusion for the VP Data Architecture. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.challenge: This Knowledge KSA was excluded as an edge case, yet it has a composite score of 0.434 (moderate signal). Knowledge-dimension KSAs represent conceptual awareness rather than execution; exclusion may be too aggressive.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.rebuttal: Edge-domain Knowledge KSAs require more than moderate signal to justify inclusion; the role's scope does not regularly engage with AI/ML Engineering. Exclusion upheld.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: Upheld: edge-domain exclusion maintained. AI/ML Engineering is not sufficiently central to the VP Data Architecture to warrant inclusion at this signal level.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world VP Data Architecture roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Job market analysis and framework role definitions confirm AI/ML Engineering is not a standard competency expectation for VP Data Architecture. Edge-case exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence does not support including this edge-domain KSA in the VP Data Architecture competency profile. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Insufficient signal (score 0.434) and edge-domain placement (AI/ML Engineering) do not meet inclusion threshold for the VP Data Architecture. All passes support exclusion.
